title: Implement TUI Responsiveness Runtime Updates
description: Update the TUI runtime in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` to default to manual refresh, expose explicit timed refresh toggling, move list/detail interaction onto scroll-native Textual behavior where possible, strengthen focus and status clarity, make mouse click and wheel routing deterministic, and reduce unnecessary full-panel rerenders by isolating list/detail/status updates and caching expensive detail rendering.
acceptance_criteria: TUI starts in manual refresh mode and only performs timed refreshes when the operator explicitly enables them.
Status/footer text clearly indicates the current refresh or run mode and action outcomes without obscuring content.
Detail rendering uses scroll-native behavior with keyboard and wheel support, resets to the top on bead selection change, and avoids full-screen rerender churn for ordinary scroll steps.
List and detail interactions update panel-locally where possible, avoid redundant heavy recomputation, and preserve existing retry/merge/status confirmation safety flows and feature-root scoping.
Mouse clicks and wheel input consistently focus and operate on the intended panel without accidental mode changes.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated the TUI runtime to start in manual mode, added explicit timed refresh control alongside timed scheduler mode, cached visible bead/detail state to avoid redundant recomputation, reset detail scroll on bead changes, routed detail scrolling through Textual widget scrolling instead of full content slicing during ordinary scroll steps, reduced rerenders to panel-local updates where possible, and kept existing merge/retry/status flows intact.
handoff_summary.risks: Behavior now depends more directly on Textual widget scrolling APIs for the detail panel, but targeted TUI and orchestrator test coverage passed.
handoff_summary.next_action: Hand off for review; optional manual terminal smoke test of `orchestrator tui` can validate the interaction feel.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low. The TUI status panel now carries explicit mode/focus text and the `a` key is reserved for timed refresh toggling; review any downstream assumptions about TUI copy or keybindings against the runtime help text.

conflict_risks: Low. The TUI status panel now carries explicit mode/focus text and the `a` key is reserved for timed refresh toggling; review any downstream assumptions about TUI copy or keybindings against the runtime help text.
